title: Discriminated Unions, Exhaustiveness, and Result Models · Boundary Failure Triage
summary: Replace boolean status flags and ambiguous errors with explicit request, success, failure, and cancellation states. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in an exhaustive asynchronous state machine and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design variants with stable literal discriminants and variant-specific required data.
Use never assignments or exhaustive helpers to make newly added variants produce actionable diagnostics.
Represent expected domain failure as typed result variants while preserving unexpected exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Pattern Evolution evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Pattern Evolution assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Model idle → loading → success|failure for request 3. Each state owns only valid fields, stale request 2 cannot overwrite the result, and adding "cancelled" must create an exhaustiveness diagnostic until handled. Competency probe: Add, deprecate, and migrate variants with compatibility tests and consumer impact evidence.

content.text: Order work so Pattern Evolution has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
